{"query":"Indo-Pacific partners NATO Ankara summit 2026 joint statement Russia North Korea illicit military cooperation text","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1046,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.nato.int/en/about-us/official-texts-and-resources/official-texts/2026/07/08/the-ankara-summit-declaration","title":"The Ankara Summit Declaration | NATO Official text","content":"In 2025, European Allies and Canada increased their investments in core defence requirements by more than $139 billion.
Today in Ankara, we announce more than $50 billion in new procurements and commit to expanding collective manufacturing capacity and working with industry to accelerate innovation.
NATO’s deterrence and defence rest on an appropriate mix of nuclear, conventional, and missile defence capabilities, complemented by space and cyber assets.
For 2026, Allies pledge €70 billion in military equipment, assistance and training for Ukraine and affirm their sovereign commitments to sustaining at least equivalent levels in 2027."},{"url":"https://www.epc.eu/publication/annotated-2026-nato-summit-declaration/","title":"Annotated 2026 NATO Summit Declaration","content":"In this annotated version of NATO’s Ankara Declaration, EPC Senior Visiting Fellow Paul Taylor reads between the lines of the official summit text and explains what NATO leaders said, what they didn’t say, and what they meant."},{"url":"https://www.congress.gov/crs-product/R49018","title":"NATO: Issues for the July 2026 Ankara Summit | Congress.gov | Library of Congress","content":"In July 2025, NATO began to coordinate the purchase of defense equipment for Ukraine from the United States by other NATO allies, through the so-called Prioritized Ukraine Requirements List (PURL) initiative.
According to the Department of Defense, as of March 2026, NATO allies and partners had committed more than $4.8 billion in PURL-related assistance, of which about $4.2 billion had been transferred.78 A June 2026 assessment by the Atlantic Council estimated that allies had committed $5.5 billion to purchases through PURL.79
European Commission and High Representative of the Union for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy, Joint White Paper for European Defence Readiness 2030, March 19, 2025, p."},{"url":"https://foreignpolicy.com/2026/07/08/nato-ankara-summit-declaration-full-text-trump-ukraine-russia/","title":"The Ankara NATO Summit Declaration Full Text","content":"Read the text outlining agreements at the 2026 NATO summit in Turkey.
We, the Heads of State and Government of the North Atlantic Alliance, have gathered in Ankara to reaffirm our ironclad commitment to our collective defence under Article 5 of the Washington Treaty and to the transatlantic bond."},{"url":"https://erkansaka.net/2026/07/10/nato-ankara-summit-2026-turkey-diplomatic-showcase/","title":"NATO Summit in Ankara. A report. - Erkan's Field Diary","content":"8 days ago — Ankara 2026 NATO Summit showcased Turkey's diplomatic ambitions, defence industry, Ukraine commitments, and NATO's internal contradictions.
During the summit, Trump publicly said the United States would remove sanctions on Türkiye and would decide on a possible F-35 sale, while Turkish readouts emphasized Erdoğan’s confidence that the summit would produce a favorable F-35 outcome.
The official UK statement says the partnership institutionalizes deeper consultations on deterrence and defence, military cooperation, industry and technology, cyber, hybrid threats, counter-terrorism, resilience, civil preparedness, and space.
It does not foreground Gaza, Syria, the eastern Mediterranean, Turkish regional security grievances, or a specific Turkish role in mediating Ukraine.
First, the Ankara Summit Declaration reaffirmed Article 5 and the 360-degree approach, linked Russia and terrorism as the two principal threats driving implementation of The Hague’s spending commitments, and pledged €70 billion for Ukraine in 2026 with allied sovereign commitments to sustain equivalent levels in 2027."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Indo-Pacific partners NATO Ankara summit 2026 joint statement Russia North Korea illicit military cooperation text","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.nippon.com/en/news/yjj2026070800218","title":"NATO, Indo-Pacific Partners Pledge Deeper Cooperation | Nippon.com","content":"LatestIn-depthJapan DataGuideVideo/LiveJapaneseJapan GlancesImagesPeopleBlog. * NATO, Indo-Pacific Partners Pledge Deeper Cooperation. Ankara, July 7 (Jiji Press)--NATO and its Indo-Pacific Four partners agreed in a meeting on Tuesday to strengthen their coordination in areas such as defense industry cooperation and cybersecurity. NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte convened the roughly 30-minute meeting with the IP4--Japan, South Korea, Australia and New Zealand--on the sidelines of a NATO summit in the Turkish capital of Ankara. The participants exchanged views on Russia's invasion of Ukraine, China-related issues, policies toward North Korea and tensions involving Iran. Japan was represented by Foreign Minister Toshimitsu Motegi and Defense Minister Shinjiro Koizumi. Referring to military cooperation between Russia and North Korea related to the invasion of Ukraine, Motegi said, \"The security of the Euro-Atlantic and the Indo-Pacific is inseparable.\". The Atakule tower is illuminated in blue for the 2026 NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey, on July 5. 1. We, the Heads of State and Government of the North Atlantic Alliance, have gathered in Ankara to reaffirm our ironclad commitment to our collective defence under Article 5 of the Washington Treaty and to the transatlantic bond. * NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte, center, poses for a group photo at the NATO Summit Defense Industry Forum, on the sidelines of the NATO leaders’ summit in Ankara, Turkey, on July 7.","score":0.5069581,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.scmp.com/news/china/military/article/3359861/nato-indo-pacific-4-pledge-defence-tech-boost-deeper-china-russia-ties-spark-alarm","title":"Nato, Indo-Pacific 4 pledge defence, tech boost as deeper China-Russia ties spark alarm | South China Morning Post","content":"### Nato chief Mark Rutte meets top officials from Australia, Japan, New Zealand and South Korea on sidelines of Ankara summit. Nato Secretary General Mark Rutte (third left) with representatives from the  Indo-Pacific Four, (from left)  New Zealand Defence Minister Chris Penk, South Korean President Lee Jae Myung, Japanese Foreign Minister Toshimitsu Motegi and Defence Minister Shinjiro Koizumi, and Australia’s defence industry minister, Pat Conroy, in Turkish capital Ankara on Tuesday. Secretary General Mark Rutte on Tuesday met with top officials from the Indo-Pacific Four (IP4) partner nations – Australia, Japan, New Zealand and South Korea – on the sidelines of the Nato summit in Ankara. The talks featured South Korean President Lee Jae Myung and Foreign Minister Cho Hyun, Japanese Foreign Minister Toshimitsu Motegi and Defence Minister Shinjiro Koizumi, New Zealand Defence Minister Chris Penk and the Australian defence industry minister, Pat Conroy.","score":0.43065065,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.nato.int/en/what-we-do/partnerships-and-cooperation/relations-with-partners-in-the-indo-pacific-region","title":"Relations with partners in the Indo-Pacific region | NATO Topic","content":"Enter the email address you registered with and we will send you a code to reset your password. Click the button to return to the page you were on and log in with your new password. # Relations with partners in the Indo-Pacific region. NATO is strengthening dialogue and cooperation with its partners in the Indo-Pacific region – Australia, Japan, the Republic of Korea and New Zealand. NATO has stepped up cooperation with its partners in the Indo-Pacific region in the past few years, including with the first-ever participation of the Heads of State and Government of these partners in a NATO summit, at the 2022 Madrid Summit. In addition to this dialogue, two landmark documents were agreed in 2022 that enhanced NATO’s cooperation with the partners from the Indo-Pacific region. NATO’s bilateral relations with each of its partners from the Indo-Pacific region have developed over the past two decades, and have included cooperation in areas such as cyber defence, the Women, Peace and Security agenda, military interoperability, maritime security, the Science for Peace and Security programme and NATO’s engagement in Afghanistan.","score":0.42792314,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/FINANCIALnews/posts/the-financial-the-nato-leaders-ankara-summit-declaration-adopted-on-july-8-2026-/1629780555822053","title":"The FINANCIAL - The FINANCIAL — The NATO leaders’ Ankara...","content":"## The FINANCIAL's post. ### **The FINANCIAL**. The FINANCIAL — The NATO leaders’ Ankara Summit Declaration, adopted on July 8, 2026, marks one of the Alliance’s clearest strategic statements since Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine. While the declaration focuses primarily on strengthening NATO’s collective defense and supporting Ukraine, its consequences extend well beyond the Alliance’s borders. For the South Caucasus—a region situated between Russia, Türkiye, Iran and the European Union—the declaration could reshape the regional security landscape for years to come. Ankara Summit Declaration Signals Stronger NATO Posture with Implications for Caucasus and European Security - FINCHANNEL.","score":0.41634628,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.32,"request_id":"921be33a-d407-42cb-baca-058817ed969c"}
